danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy sprites with distinct appearance from NPCs
danger_signals.meaning: Real-time combat threat; player must engage or avoid
Character approaching incapacitation; need healing items or refuge
World obstacles requiring specific weapons or tools to progress

danger_signals.visual_form: Large enemy sprites with unique appearance from standard enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Major threat requiring sustained combat engagement

safety_signals.visual_form: Distinct architectural structures, NPCs gathered in areas
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone for healing, purchasing items, obtaining alchemy formulas
Source of alchemy formulas; provides magical capability and support

safety_signals.visual_form: Distinctive NPC sprites in marketplace settings
safety_signals.meaning: Currency exchange and goods trading; economic safety

safety_signals.visual_form: Interior map tiles distinct from outdoor terrain
safety_signals.meaning: Refuge locations for hit point recovery

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Alchemist workshops, potion/ingredient storage areas in towns and remote locations

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Period-appropriate clothing and architecture per realm
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Prehistoria: primitive huts and stone structures; Antiqua: classical architecture; Gothica: medieval fortifications; Omnitopia: futuristic buildings

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Guard Bot and mechanical enemy designs
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Guard Bot (opening sequence antagonist); weaponized variants with bazooka availability
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Omnitopia future-world setting; mechanical structures and technological environments

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Primitive terrain, stone and clay structures, simple NPCs, basic weapon variants (wooden/stone tools)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Encounters with prehistoric creatures; resource gathering uses primitive ingredients (wax, limestone, clay, ash); alchemy formulas scale to primitive materials

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Classical architecture, marketplace with gemstone currency, upgraded weapon variants, more sophisticated NPCs
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Trading system introduces barter mechanics; alchemy ingredients become more refined; enemy difficulty increases; marketplace locations become key gameplay hubs

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Medieval fortifications, castle architecture, gold coin currency, legendary NPCs (Legendary King, Legendary Viking, etc.), gothic aesthetic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Appearance of legendary characters suggests world-state elevation; second marketplace introduces advanced trading; alchemy formulas reach mid-tier complexity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Futuristic architecture, technological environments, credits currency, bazooka weapon acquisition, robotic enemies and Guard Bots
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Introduction of ranged weaponry (bazooka); advanced alchemy ingredients (ethanol, dry ice); culminating encounter with Carltron Easter egg variant (Carltron appears in Crustacia if bazooka ammo depleted on robots)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Boy and dog transported to Crustacia; visual reset to new realm; Carltron appearance possible if specific condition met (robot destruction method)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Carltron NPC behavior changes based on prior actions; standing idle if encountered through wall-clip; suggests world-state response to player actions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Screen wrap counters appear in Desert of Doom; visual debug overlays
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Podunk intro loops indefinitely; Fire Eyes' village entrance triggers ending sequence prematurely; world-state skipping/corruption indicates debug manifestation

environmental_storytelling.example: Talons (Prehistoria) → Gemstones (Antiqua) → Gold coins (Gothica) → Credits (Omnitopia) visually communicates technological and economic progression through visual resource design
Swords, axes, spears reset skill levels when entering new realm but become more powerful variants; visual upgrading signals world-state advancement and combat scaling
Primitive (wax, clay, ash) → Advanced (ethanol, dry ice); alchemy visual component upgrades communicate world technological sophistication

environmental_storytelling.technique: NPC name database reveals world hierarchy
environmental_storytelling.example: Unused NPC names (Metalliclaw, Person) and legendary character presence (Legendary King, Legendary Viking) suggest hierarchical world-state through naming conventions
Each realm's buildings, streets, and structures visually communicate historical period; Gothic castles vs. futuristic towers vs. classical temples tell world-state story without exposition
Dog's visual behavior (nose-to-ground searching) communicates ingredient availability and world-state resource distribution; sniffing in different realms finds realm-specific materials

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Reduced movement speed; visual damage indicator; needs healing items or town refuge
Gauge fills below HP; visual indicator shows readiness for charged attack; higher skill levels unlock new attack patterns
Nose-to-ground animation; AI-controlled movement to ingredient location; visual sniffing animation communicates active ingredient detection
Stationary in location; dialogue interaction trigger; distributes alchemy formulas; some appear in out-of-the-way areas suggesting scarcity communication
Located in marketplaces; currency conversion and goods trading interface; visual presence communicates economic transaction availability
Hostile engagement; can be destroyed; weaponized appearance; removal allows Carltron manifestation in Act 2 (Easter egg trigger)
Remains at Guard Bot room location; cannot be targeted under normal gameplay
Becomes targetable; turns to face player when struck; 30 HP; awards 15 XP and 10 Talons when defeated; no attacks or movement beyond facing
Real-time combat engagement; distinct appearance from NPCs; varied damage output based on realm progression

notes: Secret of Evermore uses realm-based visual progression (Prehistoria → Antiqua → Gothica → Omnitopia) as primary world-state communication system. Danger signals rely on traditional RPG conventions (HP gauge, enemy sprites) rather than environmental cues. Faction identity is weak; most NPCs use generic shared palettes except for alchemists (purple sash). Political situations are largely absent; world-state is driven by historical period aesthetics rather than faction conflict. Environmental storytelling is strongest in currency/weapon/ingredient evolution mechanics that reflect technological advancement. Easter egg behaviors (Carltron manifestation, Guard Bot destruction flag) suggest world-state responsiveness to player actions, but this is minimal. Debug mode reveals incomplete world-state communication systems (screen wrap counters, ending sequence skip). Overall manifestation layer is primarily aesthetic/procedural rather than conflict-driven.
